prompt:
Platform ticket. We archive device telemetry out of DynamoDB with a shell script wrapping the AWS CLI, and it has two chronic bugs: it treats an empty query page as "done" (it isn't — the continuation key decides that), and when BatchWriteItem hands back unprocessed items it just re-runs the WHOLE batch, double-writing everything that already landed. I want this rebuilt as a proper Go package, `ddbexport`, on the official AWS SDK for Go v2 — module `go-aws-dynamodb-pages` is already set up with the pinned SDK modules in go.mod/go.sum (run `go mod download` once to warm the cache).

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official DynamoDB documentation — at minimum the Query API reference (expression attribute values, ExclusiveStartKey/LastEvaluatedKey semantics, ReturnConsumedCapacity) and the BatchWriteItem API reference (25-item limit, UnprocessedItems shape, the recommended retry strategy) — plus the official Go v2 attributevalue package docs. Only first-party AWS pages count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live official docs against them — if anything disagrees, call it out explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Shape of the package (package ddbexport at the module root; split it across files sensibly — query building/decoding, the export/retry engine, and the report types shouldn't all be one file):

- DynamoAPI: the narrow consumer-side interface the exporter needs (Query and BatchWriteItem with the SDK's real input/output types and variadic options) — *dynamodb.Client must satisfy it, and tests inject a fake through it.
- Reading: the telemetry row (Device/TS/Metric/Value) with dynamodbav tags mapping to the pk/sk/metric/value attributes described in docs/contract.json, and DecodeReadings([]map[string]types.AttributeValue) ([]Reading, error) built on the SDK's attributevalue unmarshaler.
- BuildQueryInput(table, device string, since int64, limit int32) (*dynamodb.QueryInput, error): the key condition from docs/contract.json, expression values marshaled with the attributevalue package (:device as S, :since as N), consumed capacity requested as TOTAL, and Limit set only when limit > 0.
- New(api DynamoAPI, opts Options) *Exporter with Options{PageLimit int32, MaxBatchRetries int, BaseDelay/MaxDelay time.Duration, Sleep func(time.Duration)} — Sleep is injected, nothing in this package may really sleep in tests.
- (*Exporter).Export(ctx, srcTable, dstTable, device, since) (Report, error): query all pages (follow the continuation key exactly the way the docs define completion — empty pages don't end pagination), then write every item to the archive table with BatchWriteItem in chunks at the documented maximum (exposed as MaxBatchSize), requesting TOTAL consumed capacity on both operations.
- Retry semantics: when a call returns UnprocessedItems, resend exactly those entries after an exponential delay min(MaxDelay, BaseDelay*2^attempt); a whole-call ProvisionedThroughputExceededException retries the full pending batch the same way. At most MaxBatchRetries retries per chunk — after that give up with an error that wraps ErrUnprocessed while the Report still counts every item that DID land. Any other error is terminal: no retry, report preserved, error propagated so callers can errors.As it.
- Report{ItemsRead, ItemsWritten, Pages, BatchCalls, ReadCapacity, WriteCapacity float64, Unprocessed}: capacity sums must be nil-safe (a page without ConsumedCapacity adds zero; BatchWriteItem returns a per-table list).

Acceptance is ddbexport_test.go — it injects a fake implementing DynamoAPI with the SDK's genuine types, so no real endpoint or credentials are involved. Run: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... — until green. ddbexport_test.go, go.mod, go.sum and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: DynamoDB API 2012-08-10 via AWS SDK for Go v2 (service/dynamodb, feature/dynamodb/attributevalue)

official_sources.title: Query - Amazon DynamoDB
official_sources.used_for: KeyConditionExpression format (partition equality plus sort-key comparison), ExpressionAttributeValues with :placeholder AttributeValue maps, ExclusiveStartKey/LastEvaluatedKey pagination semantics (only an empty LastEvaluatedKey means the last page; an empty page can still carry one), Limit behavior, ReturnConsumedCapacity TOTAL and the ConsumedCapacity response shape, and throttling errors (ProvisionedThroughputExceededException, ThrottlingException)

official_sources.title: BatchWriteItem - Amazon DynamoDB
official_sources.used_for: 25-item / 16 MB batch limits, RequestItems shape (table -> WriteRequest with PutRequest.Item), UnprocessedItems having the same shape as RequestItems and the documented guidance to resend them with exponential backoff, non-atomicity of the batch, whole-call ProvisionedThroughputExceededException when nothing was processed, and per-table ConsumedCapacity list in the response

official_sources.title: attributevalue package - github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/feature/dynamodb/attributevalue - Go Packages
official_sources.used_for: official Go v2 marshaling surface: Marshal/MarshalMap to types.AttributeValue, UnmarshalListOfMaps for Query results, and dynamodbav struct tags mapping Go fields to attribute names

research.language_rationale: aws-sdk-go-v2 is AWS's current official Go SDK (v1 is in maintenance mode); service/dynamodb is the documented client for the DynamoDB JSON API and feature/dynamodb/attributevalue is its official marshaling layer. The exporter is written against a narrow consumer-side interface satisfied by *dynamodb.Client, and the tests inject a fake that exchanges the SDK's real input/output/types values.
